rag_rules: Insert generated AGREE annex blocks into the intended target component or implementation while preserving AADL architecture
Place assumptions and guarantees that refer to component ports in valid component type scopes
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex
generic_rules: Preserve the authoritative source of component names, ports, directions, data types, subcomponents, and connections from the input AADL model
Use case-provided thresholds and legal visible feature names for input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees
Align helper declaration types with expression types to avoid type mismatches
Do not encode AADL property associations as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants for the properties
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope; otherwise omit the unsafe clause
For output dependency contracts, relate visible outputs to visible inputs in component type annexes
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent ports in a component type contract unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
